reviewer: Codex native-size review of the 160x360 candidate, Machine Hunt, and Block Mine contact sheet

characterCraft.note: The acorn-capped knight retains distinct big and pico silhouettes, facing, gait, dodge motion, sword follow-through, and hurt/celebration poses. Gnawers retain articulated shells, legs, eyes, mandibles, brace, charge trail, stun stars, and attack-state animation at the same small-world scale as the references.

environmentCraft.note: The world now reads as three built puzzle chambers rather than a generated corridor maze: framed side reliquaries, material-specific room furniture, closed sun-gate bars, open arches, cracks, briars, transformation rings, shrine architecture, light pools, canopy shade, ambient motes, and biome shell landmarks remain legible with the HUD ignored.

levelVariety.note: Seed Glade, Creek Hollow, Hearth House, and Moon Shrine use four distinct authored obstacle compositions and different architectural materials. Each floor advances through a two-solution chamber, a keyed gate transition, a second guarded choice, and a sanctum instead of repeating a hallway with a palette swap.

animationImpact.note: The montage presents a readable sequence: room scan, red-gold charge lane, pico sidestep with CLOSE ONE follow-through, big-form parry impact, sun-gate opening beam, rainstorm, and shrine restore. The charge tell persists before commitment, while slash, gate, bloom, storm, and restore effects have measured anticipation and aftermath.

readability.note: At native 160x360, blue crack and red briar solutions sit on opposite sides of each compact room, key pips explain the locked gate, and the enemy lane makes danger predictable. No computed route, breadcrumb, dashed line, or path-probe surface is rendered; posture, facing, interactable glow, gate state, and enemy telegraph carry intent.

artDirectionCohesion.note: The storybook garden language is consistent across the acorn knight, beetle wardens, reliquary choices, biome architecture, compact room-state HUD, gold sun-gate grammar, weather act, and shrine payoff. The added tactical presentation uses the existing cream, coral, sky, leaf, and gold palette rather than debug geometry.
